name: Skill Sedimentation
description: Agent-managed skill creation, editing, and security scanning. Turns successful approaches into reusable procedural knowledge stored as SKILL.md files.

enableAutoReview.description: Enable automatic background skill review after sessions with significant tool usage. Defaults to true.

nudgeInterval.description: Minimum number of new tool-calling iterations since the last review before triggering another automatic skill review. Defaults to 15.

validAgentId.description: Restrict this plugin to specific agent IDs. Accepts a single agent ID, a comma-separated list (e.g. "coder,assistant"), "*" for all agents (same as omitted), or "" to disable for all agents. When omitted, the plugin applies to all agents.

sedimentTtlDays.description: Auto-evict sediment skills that have not been touched (created/viewed/edited/matched) for this many days. Sediments still within the grace period (see sedimentGracePeriodDays) since creation are exempt, and promoted skills (now in skills/) are never affected. Set to 0 to disable TTL eviction. Defaults to 10.

sedimentMaxCount.description: Maximum number of sediment skills retained per agent. When exceeded, oldest-touched sediments are evicted (LRU) on each successful create. Sediments within the grace period (see sedimentGracePeriodDays) and promoted skills are exempt. Set to 0 to disable LRU eviction. Defaults to 20.

sedimentGracePeriodDays.description: Protection window (in days) for newly-created sediment skills: within this period, they are exempt from BOTH TTL eviction and LRU quota eviction — giving each new sediment a fair chance to be hit by a future cross-session review and promoted to skills/. Set to 0 to disable the grace period (a brand-new sediment may then be evicted immediately if the pool is over quota). Defaults to 3.

promoteMaturationDays.description: Time-based second-hit path for promote (in days). A sediment older than this is considered MATURE: a re-match in ANY session — even within the same parent conversation that created it — can promote it. This complements the cross-parent-session path, which alone is insufficient when the parent agent has a stable single sessionKey (e.g. desktop main agent). Also unlocks legacy sediments that lack the createdInParentSession marker. Set to 0 to disable the time path (promote will then require strict cross-parent-session second-hit). Defaults to 1.
